LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN ROGERS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE EIGHTH, NINTH AND TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND OVERRULED AS TO THE ELEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THESE DEFENDANTS ARE NOT LIABLE FOR ANY ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS ARISING OUT OF PROMISES MADE IN THE ASSET PURCHASE AGREEMENT OR PLEDGE AGREEMENT OR ANY STATEMENTS MADE BY THESE INDIVIDUALS ACTING ON BEHALF OF SOLIDUS DURING THE NEGOTIATION OF THESE AGREEMENTS. PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, FRAUD OR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION NOT ARISING OUT OF THESE AGREEMENTS AND ACTIONABLE CONCEALMENT RELATING TO THE PROMISES MADE IN THE ASSET PURCHASE AGREEMENT OR PLEDGE AGREEMENT. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD FRAUD AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION WITH THE REQUISITE PARTICULARITY, INCLUDING THE INDIVIDUAL WHO MADE THE MISREPRESENTATION, WHAT THE MISREPRESENTATION WAS, HOW IT WAS MADE, AND WHEN IT WAS MADE. PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS ARE NOT CLEARLY AND AFFIRMATIVELY TIME-BARRED. THE ELEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS ADEQUATELY PLED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

Off Calendar Mar-01-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven Sacks, Esq., of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP, Tel. 415.774.2905, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Walter Cook, Esq., of Law Offices of Walter Cook, Tel. 415.920.0423.x2, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. The court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DAVIES BELLER'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the eighth, ninth and tenth causes of action and overruled as to the eleventh cause of action. These Defendants are not liable for any alleged misrepresentations arising out of promises made in the Asset Purchase Agreement or Pledge Agreement or any statements made by these individuals acting on behalf of Solidus during the negotiation of these Agreements. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to plead, if they can do so in good faith, fraud or negligent misrepresentation not arising out of these Agreements and actionable concealment relating to the promises made in the Asset Purchase Agreement or Pledge Agreement. Moreover, Plaintiffs must plead fraud and negligent misrepresentation with the requisite particularity, including the individual who made the misrepresentation, what the misrepresentation was, how it was made, and when it was made. Plaintiffs' claims are not clearly and affirmatively time-barred. The eleventh cause of action is adequately pled. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
